section_title: Enforcing submission of returns and documents to Registrar: - (1) If a company, having made default in
content:
349. Enforcing submission of returns and documents to Registrar: - (1) If a company, having made default in
complying with any provision of this Act, which requires it to file with, deliver or send to the Registrar any return account or
other document, or to give notice to him of any matter fails to make good the default within fourteen days after the service
of a notice on the company requiring it to dos so, the Court may on an application made to the Court by member or creditor
of the company or by Registrar, make an order directing the company and officer thereof to make good the default within
such time as may be specified in the order.
(2) Any such order may provide that all costs of and incidental to the application shall be borne by the company or the
concerned officer in respect of any such default as aforesaid.
(3) Nothing in this section shall be taken to p[prejudice the operation of any then enactment imposing penalties on a
company or its officers in respect of any such default as aforesaid.,

sub_sections.text:
If a company, having made default in
complying with any provision of this Act, which requires it to file with, deliver or send to the Registrar any return account or
other document, or to give notice to him of any matter fails to make good the default within fourteen days after the service
of a notice on the company requiring it to dos so, the Court may on an application made to the Court by member or creditor
of the company or by Registrar, make an order directing the company and officer thereof to make good the default within
such time as may be specified in the order.

Any such order may provide that all costs of and incidental to the application shall be borne by the company or the
concerned officer in respect of any such default as aforesaid.

Nothing in this section shall be taken to p[prejudice the operation of any then enactment imposing penalties on a
company or its officers in respect of any such default as aforesaid.,
